item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want to see them test it with a d-class who doesnt have any worldly possesseions

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would just like to remind everyone that this exists, and is still wonderfully bizarre and amazing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Withholding my upvote for the moment, because I want the article's rating to stay at 108 for a little while longer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"If you like it so much, why don't you eat it?"

I think I will!
HERE AT ASCENSION BURGER!

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: Sometimes, the truth is just good!
This dialogue is fukken' good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like how a fast food restaurant was blended with a religious aspect, and the interview logs execute this concept well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think eating a mashed TV would do more than just hurt. Between the glass, shards of plastic, assorted heavy metals, acids (if there is a battery), and litany of other poisons; I'm fairly sure eating any electronics would kill you rather quickly.

But I still like it. Feels like the Fifthists started a new side-line to appeal to the masses.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Amusing. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Changed some stuff based on these suggestions!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really want to create a logo for this, the idea is simply too awesome for me to pass up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it, so upvoted. There's a few niggling little things though:

SCP-1944-1 instances are humanoid entities which resemble individuals from the ages of seventeen (17) to twenty-two (22)…

Those ages seem far too arbitrary. How would we exactly pinpoint an entity's apparent age? I think it'd work better if you said something like "which resemble individuals in their late teens or early twenties". But YMMV.

Ordering an item off the menu will result in the SCP-1944-1 instance leaving for a period ranging from three (3) to six (6) minutes to allegedly prepare the meal. SCP-1944-1 instances allegedly prepare the item in the kitchen, but any attempt at observation of the kitchen area during this process will cause SCP-1944 instances to stop working until the observation has ceased.

I'd drop one of the 'allegedly' occurrences.

Other than that, my kind of SCP. Now for me to get some inspiration up in this bitch so I can write some more of my own…
Having another kid works wonders for those with too much energy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: alright, I'll bite (gah!). it's not perfect, but I like what's here enough to hit the +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, fuck it. I just mentioned that I wasn't a fan of philosophically-themed articles and now I'm —- not going to say eating my words, godammit.

Ascension Burger. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am uncertain if the anomaly believes its own dogma, or if it is just trolling humanity, and that uncertainty just adds to my enjoyment of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What would happen if we deeded to a D-Class a gingerbread house?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks Tahony, this one's so much fun!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is so strangely…relatable in just the most obscure ways.

This is a new favorite. Wish I could +1 more than once.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is great

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, this was a joy to read from start to finish. And it's creepy as hell to boot!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1. Can I just get an everything bagel in here and be done with it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, Sorts! That means a lot! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yo, enlightenment is cool and everything, but, uh, I just wanted help with my burger

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm pretty sure all burger chains are like this though, I could have sworn the burger I had yesterday tasted like my cat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a strong competitor for my new favorite SCP

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fix'd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've changed the word foodstuff and gotten rid of the quotes. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think I'll hit the drive-thru in my Jesus Chrysler.

Wait, what? No drive-thru?

Aw, dammit…

Anyway, there is a self-contradiction. The second interviewee claims kids eat free, while the menu above seems to indicate kids only get free drinks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this.
It's creepy rather than funny, if you ask me though. Especially the constant mentions of lady with no skin (and overall, rejection of skin) in the last interview make it really work well.

At Ascension Burger I was so enlightened I almost caught fire.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At the end of the first interview where the interviewer says, "We are getting nowhere. End the interview." That's a very awkward sentence, and it's almost a break in the tone. Other then that you have made me a bit scared of eating my lunch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You're missing the "Throw Off The Shackles Of Materialism Salad" for those on a diet!

Upvoted!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Similarly, I was expecting to hate this, but it ended up just being the most bizarre thing I've felt compelled to upvote in a while.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An End to the Limitless Self-Worshiping Ego of the Human Race Cola (with ice)

I'm gonna give you an upvote WITH ICE!

E: Oh God, those interviews are totally great. I felt some temptation to say creepylarious. Thank you TheAdrift, that's the proper term to describe those interviews.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Came in really not expecting to like this, but I was laughing my balls off partway through the first interview. Wel done :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
minutes to 'prepare the meal'

I am under the distinct impression that these quotes were essentially a poor excuse to try and break tone. They can be fixed, simply by removing them.

usually describing it as 'delicious'

You don't need this part at all, especially the quotes around delicious.

foodstuff

…You can't just call it food? I mean… they're eating it and believing they're eating food. Plain and simple, not sure why one can't just replace "foodstuff" with "food" and be done with it. Maybe I just dont' understand such things.

The nature of these delusions are variable in nature, although a common theme in them is that the subject is becoming 'enlightened' or 'ascending'

The quotes as a poor excuse to use tone-breaking words, again.

Overall, I'm generally unimpressed with the effect. It just seems weird for the sake of weirdness, which I guess nothing is wrong with that, but it's not compelling in any way to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked this when you were talking about it in chat, even before I read the interviews… but DAMN. Those interviews were just the right combination of creepy and fucking hilarious. Fucking creepylarious.

+1ing this. Good job Tanhony :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This made me what in a good way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love it.

+1
